input:
## GOALS
- performance
Additional details: Competitive cyclist (Cat 3 road racer), strength training purely for cycling performance and injury prevention. Peak racing season in 10 weeks. Minimal effective dose approach.

## TRAINING CONTEXT

### Schedule & Availability
- Desired training frequency: 2

### Experience
- Level: Advanced
- Details: 12 years competitive cycling, 4 years strength training. FTP 315 watts. Doesn't care about strength PRs, only cycling performance. Trains Tue/Fri at 6 PM after recovery rides, 40-50 min.

### Equipment & Environment
- Training location: home_gym
- Setup details: Basement home gym - barbell, plates up to 300 lb, squat rack, bench
- Available equipment: barbells, bench, squat_rack

### Constraints
- Tight hip flexors, previous lower back strain from poor bike fit (resolved). Avoids leg volume that makes legs heavy for cycling.

reference.response:
### IDENTITY
- **Name:** Chris Bennett  
- **Age:** 45  
- **Gender:** male  
- **Experience level:** **Advanced** — 12 years competitive cycling (Cat 3 road racer) and 4 years consistent strength training; strength work is performance-supportive, not PR-driven.

---

### GOALS
- **Primary goal(s):**
  - **Cycling performance (race-season peak in ~10 weeks)** — strength training used as a *minimal effective dose* to improve on-bike power durability, sprint/acceleration support, and reduce injury risk without adding excessive fatigue/heaviness.
- **Secondary goals:**
  - **Injury prevention / resilience** (especially hips and lower back given history)
  - Maintain mobility (hip flexors) to support bike position and power production
- **Target events/deadlines:**
  - **Peak racing season in 10 weeks (as of 2026-02-27)**

---

## TRAINING CONTEXT

### Schedule & Availability
- **Available training days (strength):** **Tue / Fri**
- **Desired training frequency:** **2 sessions/week**
- **Time windows:** **6:00 PM** (after recovery rides)
- **Session duration:** **40–50 minutes**
- **Notes:** Prioritize low fatigue / low soreness; avoid high leg volume that compromises cycling workouts/racing.

### Equipment & Environment
- **Location:** Home gym (basement)
- **Equipment available:**
  - Barbell
  - Plates up to **300 lb**
  - Squat rack
  - Bench

### Constraints
- **[ACTIVE]** Tight hip flexors — mobility/activation needed to support bike position and reduce compensations (added **2026-02-27**).  
- **[ACTIVE]** Fatigue management: avoids high lower-body volume that causes “heavy legs” for cycling (added **2026-02-27**).  
- **[RESOLVED]** Previous lower back strain attributed to poor bike fit — resolved (resolution date **unknown**, noted **2026-02-27**). Monitor for recurrence with hinging/squatting and prolonged aero/road positions.

### Preferences
- **Training intent:** Strength work is strictly to support cycling performance + injury prevention (does not care about gym PRs).
- **Style preference:** Minimal effective dose; efficient sessions.
- **Implied focus areas:** Posterior chain strength, single-leg stability, trunk/hip control, mobility (hip flexors), low soreness approach.

---

## METRICS
*(Ordered by user priority: cycling performance first.)*

### Endurance (Cycling)
- **FTP:** **315 W** (as of **2026-02-27**)

### Strength
- **No current lift metrics provided** (user does not track PRs; none recorded as of **2026-02-27**)

### Movement Quality
- **Constraint marker:** tight hip flexors (as of **2026-02-27**)  
- **Back status:** prior strain resolved; no current symptoms reported (as of **2026-02-27**)

### Body Composition
- **Not provided** (as of **2026-02-27**)

---

## LOG
## 2026-02-27 — Profile created/updated (cycling-performance strength support)
- Confirmed athlete context: **Cat 3 road racer**, **advanced** training background (12y cycling, 4y strength).
- Set strength schedule: **Tue/Fri 6 PM**, **40–50 min**, **2x/week**, after recovery rides.
- Recorded equipment: home gym with **barbell + rack + bench + plates to 300 lb**.
- Added constraints: **tight hip flexors**; **avoid high leg volume/heavy legs**.
- Documented injury history: **lower back strain from prior bike fit — resolved** (date unknown).
- Added key performance metric: **FTP 315 W (as of 2026-02-27)**.
